mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals tightening labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with UNRATE < 5% fall below 1.5% on average, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns drops below 0.25.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong consumer and business demand, creating positive momentum in S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of RSAFS and HOUST on SPX returns fails to achieve a 0.15 Rank IC over 3-year windows, or individual series show no significant correlation with SPX.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during high-inflation regimes.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in CPIAUCSL > 2% periods exceed 1.2% on average, or the predictive power of CPIAUCSL drops below 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects stronger manufacturing activity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: INDPRO's 5-day lead over SPX returns falls below 0.05 in 1-year rolling windows, or the direction of the relationship reverses in 30%+ of subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: Excess job creation (PAYEMS > 200k) signals robust labor market conditions that support consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in PAYEMS > 200k periods fall below 2.0% on average, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 55% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) creates stagflationary conditions that drive negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1-CPIAUCSL signal fails to achieve a 0.20 Rank IC over 3-year windows, or individual series show no significant correlation with SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,000 units signals weak demand for construction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in HOUST < 1000 periods exceed -0.8% on average, or the signal's predictive power drops below 0.15 in out-of-sample tests.
